_note: Ground truth skill annotations for 9 evaluation resumes vs Ema SWE Lead ML JD. v3 — fully re-annotated from actual resume content. Explicit skills = every token visible in the Skills/Core Skills/Core Competencies section, normalised to 1-4 words. Implicit skills = demonstrated in work history only (2+ roles, central to title, or quantified outcome). Candidate IDs match RunSaver folder slugs.

_annotation_guide.explicit_skills: Every skill listed in a dedicated Skills / Core Competencies / Technical Skills section. Normalised to 1-4 word tokens. Covers all skills listed — not just JD-relevant ones. This is the ground truth the LLM extractor should reproduce.
_annotation_guide.implicit_skills: Demonstrated in work history ONLY — never from the skills section. Threshold: 2+ roles, central to job title, or quantified outcome. When in doubt, exclude.
_annotation_guide.depth_notes: Qualifications on depth (e.g. 'PyTorch (inference only)', 'Spark (basic)', 'NLP (basic)') are preserved in _depth_notes and influence whether a skill satisfies JD requirements — even if it appears in explicit_skills.
_annotation_guide.v3_changes: Built from actual PDF/DOCX resume content. Previous version (v2) was constructed from memory — missed many real skills sections tokens, particularly for R1/R2 which are much richer than annotated. R3/R4 depth qualifiers now captured. R5 and poor-match candidates correctly scoped.

R1_Arjun_Mehta_SeniorMLE._description: Staff MLE, Cohere. 7+ YoE. Deep LLM/RAG/retrieval/agent stack. IIT Bombay + CMU. 2 publications.
R1_Arjun_Mehta_SeniorMLE._depth_notes: All skills full-depth. Distributed training explicitly listed (FSDP, DeepSpeed). Fine-tuned 13B model on 32 A100s in work history.

R2_Dr_Priya_Venkataraman_AcademicCV._description: Stanford PhD + postdoc. 6 publications ACL/EMNLP. Deep RAG/retrieval/ranking/RLHF/dialogue. Limited production MLOps.
R2_Dr_Priya_Venkataraman_AcademicCV._depth_notes: No TensorFlow in skills section or work history — PyTorch and JAX only. No ETL/data-engineering background. Production MLOps (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD) absent.

R3_Sneha_Iyer_DataScientist._description: Sr. Data Scientist, Target/Groupon. 5 YoE. Classical ML + data engineering strong. NLP = TF-IDF only (explicitly labelled 'basic'). No LLMs, PyTorch, transformers, or RL.
R3_Sneha_Iyer_DataScientist._depth_notes: NLP explicitly labelled 'NLP (basic)' — TF-IDF, sentiment, LDA only. Spark explicitly 'basic'. SageMaker explicitly 'basics'. No deep learning framework in skills section. Certifications include 'LangChain for LLM Application Development (in progress)' — not completed, not counted.

R4_Marcus_Chen_MLOps._description: Sr. MLOps Engineer, Zillow. 7 YoE infra/platform. Excellent MLOps/cloud/CI-CD. Served LLMs but explicitly did NOT train them. No NLP, retrieval (ML), or RL.
R4_Marcus_Chen_MLOps._depth_notes: PyTorch listed as '(inference optimization)' only — does NOT satisfy training requirement. Elasticsearch not listed. LLM deployment (vLLM, TensorRT, ONNX) present but serving only. Resume explicitly states: 'model architecture design and training are handled by partner ML scientists.'

R5_Jake_Thompson_BackendSWE._description: Sr. Backend Engineer. 6 YoE Python/Go services. Zero ML. Explicitly targeting backend/platform roles.
R5_Jake_Thompson_BackendSWE._depth_notes: Elasticsearch listed as 'search queries only' — NOT retrieval systems in ML/IR sense. No machine learning, no NLP, no PyTorch, no model training/deployment anywhere.

business_analyst_resume_enhanced._description: Business Analyst, Accenture/Infosys. 4 YoE. SQL/Python for reporting and dashboards. No ML depth. Churn model mention has no method, framework, or outcome — insufficient for implicit ML.
business_analyst_resume_enhanced._depth_notes: Core Skills section: SQL, Python, Power BI, Tableau, Data Analysis, Requirement Gathering, Stakeholder Communication. No ML framework listed. 'Predictive model for customer churn' in achievements has no implementation detail — not counted as implicit.

frontend_developer_resume_enhanced._description: Frontend Engineer, Swiggy/Zomato. React/TS specialist. No ML, no Python, no SQL, no data overlap.
frontend_developer_resume_enhanced._depth_notes: Core Skills: JavaScript, React, TypeScript, HTML5, CSS3, Next.js, Web Performance. Entirely frontend. No Python. No SQL. No data engineering.

management_consultant_resume_enhanced._description: Senior Consultant, Deloitte/EY. Strategy + financial modeling. No ML, no Python, no data engineering.
management_consultant_resume_enhanced._depth_notes: Core Skills: Strategy, Market Research, Financial Modeling, Stakeholder Management, PowerPoint, Excel, Data Analysis. CS degree (IIT) implies basic software awareness but no evidence of ML or engineering skills in work history.

product_manager_resume_enhanced._description: PM, Flipkart/Paytm. A/B testing and SQL in product context only. No ML implementation or modelling.
product_manager_resume_enhanced._depth_notes: Core Skills: Product Strategy, Roadmapping, User Research, A/B Testing, SQL, Agile, Stakeholder Communication. A/B testing is product experimentation, not statistical ML testing. SQL is for KPI tracking.
